english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Galatians

verses.latin_vulgate: State, et nolite iterum jugo servitutis contineri.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τῇ ἐλευθερίᾳ οὖν ᾖ χριστὸς ἡμᾶς ἠλευθέρωσεν στήκετε καὶ μὴ πάλιν ζυγῷ δουλείας ἐνέχεσθε
verses.king_james: Stand fast therefore in the liberty wherewith Christ hath made us free, and be not entangled again with the yoke of bondage.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce ego Paulus dico vobis: quoniam si circumcidamini, Christus vobis nihil proderit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἴδε ἐγὼ παῦλος λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι ἐὰν περιτέμνησθε χριστὸς ὑμᾶς οὐδὲν ὠφελήσει
verses.king_james: Behold, I Paul say unto you, that if ye be circumcised, Christ shall profit you nothing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Testificor autem rursus omni homini circumcidenti se, quoniam debitor est universæ legis faciendæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μαρτύρομαι δὲ πάλιν παντὶ ἀνθρώπῳ περιτεμνομένῳ ὅτι ὀφειλέτης ἐστὶν ὅλον τὸν νόμον ποιῆσαι
verses.king_james: For I testify again to every man that is circumcised, that he is a debtor to do the whole law.

verses.latin_vulgate: Evacuati estis a Christo, qui in lege justificamini: a gratia excidistis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κατηργήθητε ἀπὸ τοῦ χριστοῦ οἵτινες ἐν νόμῳ δικαιοῦσθε τῆς χάριτος ἐξεπέσατε
verses.king_james: Christ is become of no effect unto you, whosoever of you are justified by the law; ye are fallen from grace.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nos enim spiritu ex fide, spem justitiæ exspectamus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡμεῖς γὰρ πνεύματι ἐκ πίστεως ἐλπίδα δικαιοσύνης ἀπεκδεχόμεθα
verses.king_james: For we through the Spirit wait for the hope of righteousness by faith.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam in Christo Jesu neque circumcisio aliquid valet, neque præputium: sed fides, quæ per caritatem operatur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν γὰρ χριστῷ ἰησοῦ οὔτε περιτομή τι ἰσχύει οὔτε ἀκροβυστία ἀλλὰ πίστις δι᾽ ἀγάπης ἐνεργουμένη
verses.king_james: For in Jesus Christ neither circumcision availeth any thing, nor uncircumcision; but faith which worketh by love.

verses.latin_vulgate: Currebatis bene: quis vos impedivit veritati non obedire?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐτρέχετε καλῶς τίς ὑμᾶς ἀνέκοψεν τῇ ἀληθείᾳ μὴ πείθεσθαι
verses.king_james: Ye did run well; who did hinder you that ye should not obey the truth?

verses.latin_vulgate: persuasio hæc non est ex eo, qui vocat vos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ πεισμονὴ οὐκ ἐκ τοῦ καλοῦντος ὑμᾶς
verses.king_james: This persuasion *cometh* not of him that calleth you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Modicum fermentum totam massam corrumpit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μικρὰ ζύμη ὅλον τὸ φύραμα ζυμοῖ
verses.king_james: A little leaven leaveneth the whole lump.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego confido in vobis in Domino, quod nihil aliud sapietis: qui autem conturbat vos, portabit judicium, quicumque est ille.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγὼ πέποιθα εἰς ὑμᾶς ἐν κυρίῳ ὅτι οὐδὲν ἄλλο φρονήσετε ὁ δὲ ταράσσων ὑμᾶς βαστάσει τὸ κρίμα ὅστις ἂν ᾖ
verses.king_james: I have confidence in you through the Lord, that ye will be none otherwise minded: but he that troubleth you shall bear his judgment, whosoever he be.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego autem, fratres, si circumcisionem adhuc prædico: quid adhuc persecutionem patior? ergo evacuatum est scandalum crucis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγὼ δέ ἀδελφοί εἰ περιτομὴν ἔτι κηρύσσω τί ἔτι διώκομαι ἄρα κατήργηται τὸ σκάνδαλον τοῦ σταυροῦ
verses.king_james: And I, brethren, if I yet preach circumcision, why do I yet suffer persecution? then is the offence of the cross ceased.

verses.latin_vulgate: Utinam et abscindantur qui vos conturbant.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὄφελον καὶ ἀποκόψονται οἱ ἀναστατοῦντες ὑμᾶς
verses.king_james: I would they were even cut off which trouble you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vos enim in libertatem vocati estis, fratres: tantum ne libertatem in occasionem detis carnis, sed per caritatem Spiritus servite invicem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑμεῖς γὰρ ἐπ᾽ ἐλευθερίᾳ ἐκλήθητε ἀδελφοί μόνον μὴ τὴν ἐλευθερίαν εἰς ἀφορμὴν τῇ σαρκί ἀλλὰ διὰ τῆς ἀγάπης δουλεύετε ἀλλήλοις
verses.king_james: For, brethren, ye have been called unto liberty; only *use* not liberty for an occasion to the flesh, but by love serve one another.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnis enim lex in uno sermone impletur: Diliges proximum tuum sicut teipsum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ γὰρ πᾶς νόμος ἐν ἑνὶ λόγῳ πληροῦται, ἐν τῷ ἀγαπήσεις τὸν πλησίον σου ὡς εαυτόν
verses.king_james: For all the law is fulfilled in one word, *even* in this; Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod si invicem mordetis, et comeditis: videte ne ab invicem consumamini.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δὲ ἀλλήλους δάκνετε καὶ κατεσθίετε βλέπετε μὴ ὑπό ἀλλήλων ἀναλωθῆτε
verses.king_james: But if ye bite and devour one another, take heed that ye be not consumed one of another.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dico autem: Spiritu ambulate, et desideria carnis non perficietis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγω δέ πνεύματι περιπατεῖτε καὶ ἐπιθυμίαν σαρκὸς οὐ μὴ τελέσητε
verses.king_james: *This* I say then, Walk in the Spirit, and ye shall not fulfil the lust of the flesh.

verses.latin_vulgate: Caro enim concupiscit adversus spiritum, spiritus autem adversus carnem: hæc enim sibi invicem adversantur, ut non quæcumque vultis, illa faciatis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ γὰρ σὰρξ ἐπιθυμεῖ κατὰ τοῦ πνεύματος τὸ δὲ πνεῦμα κατὰ τῆς σαρκός ταῦτα δὲ ἀντίκειται ἀλλήλοις ἵνα μὴ ἃ ἂν θέλητε ταῦτα ποιῆτε
verses.king_james: For the flesh lusteth against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh: and these are contrary the one to the other: so that ye cannot do the things that ye would.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod si Spiritu ducimini, non estis sub lege.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δὲ πνεύματι ἄγεσθε οὐκ ἐστὲ ὑπὸ νόμον
verses.king_james: But if ye be led of the Spirit, ye are not under the law.

verses.latin_vulgate: Manifesta sunt autem opera carnis, quæ sunt fornicatio, immunditia, impudicitia, luxuria,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: φανερὰ δέ ἐστιν τὰ ἔργα τῆς σαρκός ἅτινά ἐστιν μοιχεία, πορνεία ἀκαθαρσία ἀσέλγεια
verses.king_james: Now the works of the flesh are manifest, which are *these*; Adultery, fornication, uncleanness, lasciviousness,

verses.latin_vulgate: idolorum servitus, veneficia, inimicitiæ, contentiones, æmulationes, iræ, rixæ, dissensiones, sectæ,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰδωλολατρεία φαρμακεία ἔχθραι ἔρεις, ζῆλοι, θυμοί ἐριθείαι διχοστασίαι αἱρέσεις
verses.king_james: Idolatry, witchcraft, hatred, variance, emulations, wrath, strife, seditions, heresies,

verses.latin_vulgate: invidiæ, homicidia, ebrietates, comessationes, et his similia, quæ prædico vobis, sicut prædixi: quoniam qui talia agunt, regnum Dei non consequentur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: φθόνοι φόνοι, μέθαι κῶμοι καὶ τὰ ὅμοια τούτοις ἃ προλέγω ὑμῖν καθὼς καὶ προεῖπον ὅτι οἱ τὰ τοιαῦτα πράσσοντες βασιλείαν θεοῦ οὐ κληρονομήσουσιν
verses.king_james: Envyings, murders, drunkenness, revellings, and such like: of the which I tell you before, as I have also told *you* in time past, that they which do such things shall not inherit the kingdom of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fructus autem Spiritus est caritas, gaudium, pax, patientia, benignitas, bonitas, longanimitas,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ καρπὸς τοῦ πνεύματός ἐστιν ἀγάπη χαρά εἰρήνη μακροθυμία χρηστότης ἀγαθωσύνη πίστις
verses.king_james: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith,

verses.latin_vulgate: mansuetudo, fides, modestia, continentia, castitas. Adversus hujusmodi non est lex.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πρᾳότης, ἐγκράτεια κατὰ τῶν τοιούτων οὐκ ἔστιν νόμος
verses.king_james: Meekness, temperance: against such there is no law.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui autem sunt Christi, carnem suam crucifixerunt cum vitiis et concupiscentiis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ τοῦ χριστοῦ τὴν σάρκα ἐσταύρωσαν σὺν τοῖς παθήμασιν καὶ ταῖς ἐπιθυμίαις
verses.king_james: And they that are Christ's have crucified the flesh with the affections and lusts.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si Spiritu vivimus, Spiritu et ambulemus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ ζῶμεν πνεύματι πνεύματι καὶ στοιχῶμεν
verses.king_james: If we live in the Spirit, let us also walk in the Spirit.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non efficiamur inanis gloriæ cupidi, invicem provocantes, invicem invidentes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ γινώμεθα κενόδοξοι ἀλλήλους προκαλούμενοι ἀλλήλοις φθονοῦντες
verses.king_james: Let us not be desirous of vain glory, provoking one another, envying one another.
